growth_records.cite: Wecker P et al. Transcriptional response of the model planctomycete Rhodopirellula baltica SH1T to changing environmental conditions. BMC Genomics (2009). DOI:10.1186/1471-2164-10-410. [PMC2754498]
growth_records.snippet: For all experiments Rhodopirellula baltica SH1T cells were grown as chemostat cultures in a mineral medium containing 10 mM glucose as the sole carbon source and 1 mM ammonium chloride as a nitrogen source at 28 C. Under optimal conditions
growth_records.notes: Prokaryote (planctomycete bacterium) grown as a glucose-limited chemostat in defined mineral medium (10 mM glucose + 1 mM NH4Cl, 28C) for a transcriptomics stress study. NO uptake or secretion RATES are measured/reported in this paper. Doubling time entered as 11 h (midpoint of the reported 10-12 h) but this value is CITED from another study, ref [21] (Gade et al., proteome of R. baltica), not measured here: 'Under optimal conditions R. baltica has a doubling time of 10-12 hours [21]'. Optimal growth temperature 28C is cited from Schlesner ref [35]. Chemostat dilution rate (D) is not stated, so growth_rate_per_h left null (cannot compute mu=D). doubling_time_h therefore reflects a literature-cited physiological value, not an in-paper measurement. Record retained for the defined growth condition only; downstream users should treat the doubling time as an external citation.
